MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556
  • Home

  • Release Notes
AudioCapture Example C++

AudioCapture illuminates the general work flow of how to capture LPCM audio and how to call various interfaces to complete the task. You can take this example for reference to make application for your own sessions.
Note that this example is only supported by Pro and Eco capture family.
Folder dir: SDKv3\Examples\Legacy_Legacy_VC++\CmdTools\AudioCapture

Supported Hardware

Pro Capture Family, Eco Capture Family

Features

  1. Capture audio data from the input signal.
  2. Save LPCM audio signal as .wav files.
  3. Specify the capture channel when multiple devices are loaded in your system.

Workflow

  1. To get device version, initialize and enumerate all available devices, call: MWGetVersion, MWCaptureInitInstance, MWGetChannelCount;
  2. To find the valid capture card according to the device info, call: MWGetChannelInfoByIndex

  3. Conduct any of the following steps to open a channel:

    1. To open specified channel by the command parameter, call: MWOpenChannel;
    2. To open the first available channel by default, call: MWGetDevicePath, MWOpenChannelByPath;
  4. To get the number and status of the input audio source, call: MWGetAudioInputSourceArray, MWGetAudioSignalStatus
  5. To start audio capture, call: MWStartAudioCapture
  6. To register for change notifications, call: MWRegisterNotify
  7. To capture an audio frame triggered by specified notification, call: MWGetNotifyStatus, MWCaptureAudioFrame
  8. Save the data in .wav format.
  9. Repeat step 7 and 8.
  10. To stop recording, free up system resources and memory, call: MWUnregisterNotify, MWStopAudioCapture, MWCloseChannel, MWCaptureExitInstance

Result

AudioCapture
